reasoning: While programmatic signals show high text recall (0.973), the LLM fidelity score (0.84) is below the auto-approval threshold due to major structural and visual defects. Specifically, the loss of significant branding and contextual images (FEMA, National Preparedness Month) and localized text duplication/corruption on page 5 require manual correction. Because a bad document auto-approved is 3x more costly than a review, these visual gaps and OCR artifacts necessitate human intervention.

concerns.analysis: The output contains a duplicated text fragment within a single paragraph element in Section 5. The phrase 'Authentication and Access Control' is repeated twice in the event listing for September 7, whereas it only appears once in the corresponding line on page 5 of the source PDF. This seems to be a structural artifact where text from the line was concatenated twice during the extraction process.
